explanation.label: Java management extensions API
explanation.description: JMX (Java Management Extensions) is a set of APIs and tools for managing and monitoring Java applications. It allows developers to create custom management agents that can be used to monitor and manage Java applications. JMX is based on the Java Management Extensions (JMX) API, which provides a standard way for Java applications to expose their management data and operations. The JMX API provides a set of interfaces and classes that can be used to create management agents, manage MBeans (Java objects that represent management data), and access management data. JMX is widely used in enterprise Java applications for monitoring and managing system resources, such as memory, CPU, and network usage. It is also used for monitoring and managing application-specific data, such as database connections and transaction logs.
